industries.name: Flame Retardants
uses: Brominated flame retardants (BFRs) added to polymer resins and textiles to inhibit ignition and slow flame spread — bromine radical chemistry interrupts the combustion chain reaction in the gas phase

Tetrabromobisphenol A (TBBPA): the highest-volume brominated flame retardant, primarily used as reactive flame retardant in epoxy resin for FR-4 printed circuit board laminates — virtually every PCB in computers, smartphones, and appliances contains TBBPA

Decabromodiphenyl ether (DecaBDE) historically used in TV and computer housings, carpet backings, and textiles; being phased out globally under Stockholm Convention due to persistence and bioaccumulation concerns
Hexabromocyclododecane (HBCDD) used in expanded polystyrene (EPS) insulation boards — restricted under Stockholm Convention; being replaced by polymeric flame retardants
Novel polymeric brominated flame retardants (PolyFR, PDBS) designed to be non-bioaccumulative alternatives to legacy BFRs in polymers, electronics, and textiles

companies.name: ICL Group (ICL Bromine)
companies.use: World's largest bromine producer. Extracts bromine from Dead Sea brines at Neot Hovav, Israel. Produces TBBPA, brominated epoxy resins, and specialty bromine compounds for electronics flame retardants and other applications.

companies.name: Albemarle Corporation
companies.use: Second-largest bromine producer. Extracts bromine from brine wells in Arkansas (Smackover Formation) and Jordan. Major producer of SAYTEX and SAYTEX HP brominated flame retardants for electronics, construction, and transportation.

companies.name: Lanxess
companies.use: Major specialty chemicals producer manufacturing brominated flame retardants, biocides, and specialty bromine compounds. Produces EMERALD Innovation 3000 polymeric flame retardant as a safer alternative to legacy BFRs.

industries.name: Oil & Gas Drilling Fluids
uses: Zinc bromide (ZnBr₂), calcium bromide (CaBr₂), and sodium bromide (NaBr) as clear, dense completion and workover fluids in oil and gas well completions — high density (up to 19.2 lb/gal for ZnBr₂) provides wellbore pressure control without suspended solids that could damage the reservoir
Bromide brines used as packer fluids in the annulus of completed wells, preventing reservoir fluids from migrating up the wellbore during production

Bromide workover fluids allow well intervention (equipment installation, logging, stimulation) while maintaining pressure control using clear fluid rather than weighted solids-laden muds that risk formation damage
Sodium bromide (NaBr) used to produce bromine on-site for water treatment and biocide applications in offshore platforms and onshore oil field water handling

companies.name: ICL Group (TETRA Technologies)
companies.use: Dominant supplier of calcium bromide, zinc bromide, and sodium bromide completion fluids to offshore and onshore oil and gas well operators globally. ICL produces the bromine; TETRA Technologies formulates and delivers completion fluids.

companies.name: Albemarle Corporation
companies.use: Major producer of oilfield bromide brines and completion fluids from its Arkansas brine operations. Supplies CaBr₂ and NaBr fluids to oil and gas operators in the Gulf of Mexico, North Sea, and Middle East.

industries.name: Water Treatment Biocides & Agriculture
uses: Bromine-based biocides (BCDMH, DBDMH) for industrial cooling water treatment — more effective than chlorine at higher pH and against biofilm-forming bacteria; used in power plant cooling towers, paper mills, and HVAC systems
Sodium bromide + oxidant (Cl₂ or ClO₂) generates bromine in situ for swimming pool and spa disinfection — bromine is less irritating to eyes and more effective at higher pH than chlorine

Methyl bromide (CH₃Br) as soil fumigant for pre-plant treatment of strawberry fields, tomato nurseries, and ornamental plant production — being phased out under Montreal Protocol (ozone-depleting substance); critical use exemptions still allowed in some countries
Brominated biocides for ballast water treatment in ships — preventing transfer of invasive species across ocean basins in ship ballast water is mandated by IMO Ballast Water Convention

companies.name: Albemarle Corporation
companies.use: Major producer of BCDMH (1-bromo-3-chloro-5,5-dimethylhydantoin) and other bromine-based biocides for industrial water treatment and pool/spa sanitization. Also produces silver-based antimicrobial zeolites.

companies.name: ICL Group
companies.use: Major producer of bromine-based industrial biocides, pool chemicals, and agricultural fumigants. Its DBNPA, DBDMH, and NaBr products are widely used in industrial water treatment and recreational water sanitization.

industries.name: Pharmaceuticals & Photography
uses: Bromine is a key building block in pharmaceutical synthesis — approximately 30+ marketed drugs contain bromine including loratadine (Claritin), rocuronium bromide (anesthetic), and bromhexine (expectorant)
Lithium bromide and potassium bromide as historically important sedatives and anticonvulsants; potassium bromide still used in veterinary medicine for epilepsy in dogs

Silver bromide (AgBr) as the primary light-sensitive material in traditional photographic film and paper — photons reduce Ag⁺ to metallic silver clusters forming the latent image in silver halide emulsion
Hydrobromic acid (HBr) as reagent in pharmaceutical synthesis for alkyl bromide preparation, salt formation of basic drugs, and as a catalyst in specialty chemical processes

companies.name: Pfizer
companies.use: Major pharmaceutical company using bromine intermediates in synthesis of active pharmaceutical ingredients. Rocuronium bromide (anesthetic) and several other Pfizer drugs contain bromine as part of their molecular structure.

companies.name: Organon (Merck spinoff)
companies.use: Pharmaceutical company producing brominated active pharmaceutical ingredients including ipratropium bromide (Atrovent, COPD inhaler) and tiotropium bromide (Spiriva), both bromine-containing anticholinergic bronchodilators.
